dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7225 IH: Protecting Child Sex Trafficking Victim Witnesses Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Moore of North Carolina (for himself and Mr. Correa) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To strengthen protections for child sex trafficking victims testifying against human traffickers.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Protecting Child Sex Trafficking Victim Witnesses Act.

subsection.text: Not later than 18 months after the date of enactment of this Act, the Attorney General, in consultation with Federal law enforcement agencies and prosecutors and other appropriate stakeholders, shall develop and publish victim-centered guidance to strengthen protections for child sex trafficking victims testifying against human traffickers, including—

paragraph.text: practices to minimize the adverse consequences of testifying on child sex trafficking victim witnesses;
safe travel, lodging, and accompaniment for child sex trafficking victim witnesses;
use of child advocacy centers and family justice centers, where appropriate; and
safety planning, including post-trial safety planning.

subsection.text: Not later than 180 days after the publication of the guidance required under subsection (a), the Attorney General shall disseminate and provide training to each office of the United States Attorney on the victim-centered guidance.

subsection.header: Department of Justice training and policy for law enforcement officers, prosecutors, and judges
subsection.text: Section 209(c)(1)(A) of the Trafficking Victims Protection Reauthorization Act of 2005 (34 U.S.C. 20709(c)(1)(A)) is amended—

paragraph.text: in clause (iii), by striking and at the end;
in clause (iv)(II), by striking the period at the end and inserting ; and; and
by adding at the end the following:

clause.text: protections for child sex trafficking victims testifying against human traffickers based on the victim-centered guidance published by the Attorney General in accordance with section 2(a) of the Protecting Child Sex Trafficking Victim Witnesses Act.

subsection.text: Section 209(c)(1)(B) of the Trafficking Victims Protection Reauthorization Act of 2005 (34 U.S.C. 20709(c)(1)(B)) is amended—

paragraph.text: by striking training on seeking and inserting the following:
by striking the period at the end and inserting ; and; and
by adding at the end the following:

clause.text: victim-centered guidance to strengthen protections for child sex trafficking victims testifying against human traffickers based on victim-centered guidance published by the Attorney General in accordance with section 2(a) of the Protecting Child Sex Trafficking Victim Witnesses Act.

section.header: Additional uses for victim-centered child human trafficking deterrence block grant
section.text: Section 203(b)(1) of the Trafficking Victims Protection Reauthorization Act of 2005 (34 U.S.C. 20703(b)(1)) is amended—

paragraph.text: in subparagraph (D), by striking and at the end;
by adding at the end the following:

subparagraph.text: strengthen protections for child sex trafficking victims testifying against human traffickers based on victim-centered guidance published by the Attorney General in accordance with section 2(a) of the Protecting Child Sex Trafficking Victim Witnesses Act; and
carry out any of the authorized uses of a grant under section 204(a)(1) of the Trafficking Victims Protection Reauthorization Act of 2005.

section.header: Modernizing Federal child victims’ and child witnesses’ rights
section.text: Section 3509 of title 18, United States Code, is amended—

subparagraph.text: in paragraph (6), by striking child prostitution and inserting child sex trafficking and child prostitution;
in paragraph (7), by inserting victim advocacy, after law enforcement,; and
in paragraph (8), by striking prostitution and inserting commercial sex act (as defined in section 103 of the Trafficking Victims Protection Act of 2000 (22 U.S.C. 7102));
in paragraph (1)(A), in the first sentence, by striking in a room; and

clause.text: in the paragraph heading, by after Videotaped the following: and Digitally recorded;
in subparagraph (A), by inserting or digital recording after on videotape;

subclause.text: in clause (ii), by inserting or digital recording after by videotape;

item.text: in the first sentence, by inserting after videotape the following: or digitally recorded; and
in subclause (IV), by inserting after videotape the following: or recording; and
in the clause heading, by inserting after videotape the following: or digital recording;
in the first sentence, by inserting or digital recording after on video tape; and
in the second sentence, by inserting after videotape the following: or digital recording;

clause.text: in subparagraph (C), by inserting after videotaped the following: or digitally recorded;

subclause.text: by inserting after videotaping the following: or digital recording; and
by inserting after videotaped the following: or digitally recorded;

clause.text: in subparagraph (E), by inserting after videotaped the following: or digitally recorded; and
in subparagraph (F), by inserting after videotape each place it appears the following: or digital recording; and

paragraph.text: in subsection (i), in the last sentence, by inserting after recorded on videotape the following: or digitally recorded.
